You can declare a variable without specifying its type (e.g., `var x`).

childMdx.rawBody:
Before Java 10, **every** variable declaration
(local variables, formal parameters, instance variables, and class variables)
had to explicitly specify the type of that variable.

Starting with Java 10, *local variable declarations* do not need
to specify the type anymore (they can use `var name = ...;`).

However, it is important to understand that Java is statically typed,
and all its variables have to be declared,
and their type has to be determined statically (at compile time).

In Java 10, `var` was added to declare local variables
without having to explicitly specify their type,
allowing developers to rely on the Java compiler
to (statically) infer the type of local variables.
